title: Senior Software Engineer I
description:
Goldcast (a Cvent company) is an AI\\-powered B2B Video Content Platform that enables marketers to put video content at the heart of the customer journey. We’re building a
 **browser\\-based, frame\\-accurate video editor** 
 that rivals desktop apps, using cutting\\-edge tech like WebCodecs, WASM, and Remotion.
 



  





 Founded in mid\\-2020 at Harvard Business School, we have 400\\+ customers across tech, professional services, manufacturing, and finance to deliver engaging video content \\& digital events.
 



  





**Role Overview**




  





 We're seeking a
 **Senior Software Engineer** 
 to join our
 **Recording Studio** 
 team. You will be responsible for leading the development of high\\-performance, scalable backend systems that power Goldcast’s recording and video processing platform.
 



  





 The ideal candidate will drive technical excellence, make strong architectural decisions within their project scope, and help improve the reliability, scalability, and performance of our recording and media processing infrastructure.
 



  





 In this role, you'll lead and deliver complex backend features involving multiple engineers, taking work from definition through design, implementation, testing, and production rollout. You will collaborate closely with product, design, QA, and SRE, and contribute to the reliability and performance of our production systems.
 



  





 You'll work on initiatives across the Recording Studio surface area, such as improving recording session management, enhancing media ingestion and processing pipelines, optimizing HLS generation and trimming workflows, and strengthening integrations with Content Lab and Video Library.
 



  





**In This Role, You Will:**




  




* Lead and deliver complex backend features end\\-to\\-end as the feature lead
* Break down ambiguous backend and distributed systems problems into clear milestones and execution plans
* Participate in system design and write clear, pragmatic design docs
* Build and maintain backend services for recording session lifecycle, media ingestion, and asset processing
* Design and optimize video processing workflows, including chunk management, transcoding, and publishing
* Improve system observability, reliability, and failure recovery mechanisms
* Debug issues across services, storage systems, and processing pipelines, and drive fixes through to resolution
* Contribute to code reviews and team engineering practices
* Participate in the on\\-call rotation and help improve production reliability over time



  





**Requirements:**




  




* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science or related field from a top\\-tier institution (IITs, NITs, BITs, or equivalent).
* 6\\+ years of professional software engineering experience
* Strong fundamentals in data structures, algorithms, and system design
* Experience building and operating scalable backend systems in production
* Ability to lead medium\\-to\\-large features with ambiguity and drive them to completion
* Experience working with distributed systems and asynchronous processing
* Familiarity with cloud environments (AWS preferred)
* Experience with object storage systems (e.g., S3\\) and large file processing
* Experience with message queues or event\\-driven architectures
* Experience with video processing, HLS streams, or media pipelines is a strong plus
* Strong debugging skills and experience handling production incidents

title: Senior Software Engineer II - Video Engineering
description:
Goldcast (a Cvent company) is an AI\\-powered B2B Video Content Platform that enables marketers to put video content at the heart of the customer journey. We’re building a
 **browser\\-based, frame\\-accurate video editor**

 Founded in mid\\-2020 at Harvard Business School, we have 400\\+ customers across tech, professional services, manufacturing, and finance to deliver engaging video content \\& digital events.

 We’re seeking a highly skilled Senior Software Engineer II (SSE\\-II) to join our Content Lab — the team pioneering AI\\-driven video transformation at scale.
 



  





 In this role, you’ll lead complex backend projects, focusing on distributed systems, real\\-time video processing, and media pipeline scalability. You’ll partner with product, design, and QA to deliver high\\-quality, maintainable solutions that enhance our AI\\-enabled video content platform.
 



  





 This role requires deep technical expertise in video streaming technologies, backend architecture, and system performance, as well as a passion for mentoring engineers and driving engineering excellence.
 



  





**In This Role, You Will:**




  





**Technical Ownership \\& Leadership** 



* Lead large, cross\\-functional projects involving multiple engineers, driving design, architecture, and execution from concept to production rollout.
* Design and implement high\\-performance backend systems that support video encoding, transcoding, and adaptive streaming at scale.
* Make architectural and design decisions that balance scalability, maintainability, and performance.



  





**Video Systems Engineering** 



* Work with frameworks like FFmpeg, GStreamer, or similar to build efficient video pipelines handling multiple codecs (H.264, H.265, VP9, AV1\\) and formats (MP4, WebM, HLS, DASH).
* Optimize video storage, retrieval, and delivery performance through CDN, caching, and adaptive bitrate streaming strategies.
* Collaborate on integrating cloud media services (AWS Media Services, Azure Media Services, or GCP Transcoder API) into our stack.

**Operational Excellence** 



* Troubleshoot and resolve production issues efficiently, performing deep root\\-cause analyses and implementing long\\-term fixes.
* Improve observability, logging, and monitoring to strengthen production reliability.
* Champion engineering excellence through documentation, testing, and continuous improvement initiatives.



  





  





**Here's What You Need:**




  




* 8\\+ years of backend engineering experience in large\\-scale distributed systems or video processing environments.
* Strong command of Python, Go, or Java.
* Deep understanding of video codecs, compression techniques, and streaming protocols (HLS, DASH, RTMP, WebRTC).
* Proven experience with FFmpeg, GStreamer, or similar multimedia frameworks.

* Architecture \\& Scaling: Expertise in designing scalable microservices, distributed APIs, and performance optimization.

* Communication: Excellent written and verbal English communication skills, with the ability to work across global teams.
* Education: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related discipline (from IITs, NITs, BITs, or equivalent institutions preferred).

title: Platform Test Lead
description:
Looking for Your Dream Job?
   

  

 Join Our Ice Cream Team!
   

  

**Role:** 
 Platform Test Lead (SAP \\& Non\\-SAP)
   

  

**Location:** 
 Ice Cream Office Pune
   

  

**Scope:** 
 Global
   

  

**Type:** 
 Full\\-time
   

  

**Position Summary**
 We are seeking a highly technical and strategic Platform Test Architect to define and lead the testing strategy for our enterprise application landscape, which includes SAP (S/4HANA, ECC, BTP) and connected non\\-SAP applications. The ideal candidate will be an expert in driving end\\-to\\-end automation, performance engineering, and service virtualization, ensuring high quality and rapid delivery in DevOps pipelines.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Strategy \\& Architecture: Design, implement, and maintain a comprehensive testing strategy for hybrid, multi\\-application environments, incorporating both SAP and non\\-SAP systems.
* Tool Expertise: Act as the subject matter expert (SME) for Tricentis qTest (test management), Tricentis NeoLoad (performance), and Int4 Suite (API/Interface testing).
* Int4 \\& SAP Testing: Leverage Int4 Suite for automated testing of SAP middleware (SAP PI/PO, SAP Cloud Integration, SAP AIF), enabling virtualized testing without needing agent installations.
* Performance Engineering: Define and execute performance testing strategies (load, stress, endurance) for complex, integrated SAP and non\\-SAP scenarios using NeoLoad.
* Automation Frameworks: Build and maintain scalable, model\\-based test automation frameworks (incorporating Tosca/qTest).
* DevOps Integration: Integrate qTest and NeoLoad into CI/CD pipelines (e.g., Jenkins, Azure DevOps) for continuous testing.
* Test Data Management: Develop test data management strategies to ensure data readiness across SIT, regression, and performance cycles.
* Governance \\& Mentorship: Establish governance processes for test script maintenance and provide training/coaching to QA teams.
* Test Strategy \\& Planning: Develop and implement comprehensive test strategies and plans for ERP projects including but not limited to SAP4Hana, including integration, system, regression, and user acceptance testing (UAT) across the total deployment landscape.
* Team Leadership: Co\\-ordinate a team of test analysts (onshore and offshore), ensuring effective test execution and timely delivery of project milestones.
* Test Design \\& Execution: Oversee the creation and review of test cases, test scripts, and test data based on business requirements and functional specifications.
* Defect Management: Manage defect lifecycle, including logging, prioritisation, tracking, and resolution in collaboration with development and functional teams.
* Stakeholder Communication: Act as the primary point of contact for all testing activities, providing regular updates to project managers, business stakeholders, and technical teams.
* Quality Assurance: Ensure compliance with quality standards, best practices, and audit requirements throughout the testing process.
* Tool Utilisation: Utilise SAP testing tools (e.g., SAP Solution Manager, HP ALM, Tricentis Tosca , INT4 ) to manage test assets and reporting.
* Continuous Improvement: Identify opportunities to improve testing processes and methodologies for future ERP and non EPR projects.
* Preferred Skills


**Tooling**
* Int4 Suite: Deep knowledge of SAP interface testing and service virtualization.
* Tricentis NeoLoad: Expert\\-level performance testing for SAP HANA, Fiori, and web services.
* Tricentis qTest: Experience managing requirements, test plans, and automation execution.
* SAP Domain Knowledge: Hands\\-on experience with S/4HANA, ECC, and SAP Activate methodology.
* Technical Skills: Proficiency in SQL, API testing (REST/SOAP), and scripting languages.
* Non\\-SAP Skills: Experience testing web apps, microservices, and third\\-party integrations.
* Proven experience as a Test Lead in large\\-scale SAP S/4HANA implementations or upgrades.
* In\\-depth understanding of SAP S/4HANA modules, architecture, and business processes.
* Strong knowledge of testing methodologies, tools, and best practices.
* Experience in managing and mentoring test teams.
* Excellent problem\\-solving, communication, and stakeholder management skills.
* Familiarity with Agile and Waterfall project delivery models.
* Experience with test automation tools is a plus.
* ISTQB or equivalent testing certification preferred.
* Tricentis Tosca certification (Model\\-based automation).
* Experience with SAP Solution Manager (SolMan) 7\\.2 and SAP Cloud ALM


**Required Qualifications**
* Experience: 10\\+ years in software testing, with at least 5\\+ years in a Lead/Architect role specializing in SAP.
* Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field (or equivalent experience).


**Leadership Skills**
* PASSION FOR HIGH PERFORMANCE: Takes personal responsibility and accountability for execution and results. Generates intensity and focus to motivate people to deliver results at speed.
* PERSONAL MASTERY: Sets high standards for themselves. Actively builds own wellbeing and resilience.
* TALENT CATALYST: Invests in people – coaches individuals and teams to realize their full potential. Creates an inclusive climate, empowering everyone to be at their best. Inspires powerful teamwork and collaboration, connecting people for better results.
* CONSUMER LOVE: Brings the voice of the consumer into everything we do, always.
* BUSINESS ACUMEN: Creates opportunities for profitable growth through the core and beyond.

title: Lead Engineer - Test Automation
description:

As one of the fastest\\-growing SaaS companies in history, we surpassed $2B in revenue in our last fiscal year with extensive growth potential ahead.
   

  

 At the heart of Veeva are our values: Do the Right Thing, Customer Success, Employee Success, and Speed. We're not just any public company – we made history in 2021 by becoming a public benefit corporation (PBC), legally bound to balancing the interests of customers, employees, society, and investors.
   

  

 Join us in transforming the life sciences industry, committed to making a positive impact on its customers, employees, and communities.
   

  

**The Role**
 Do you dream about being part of a team with the talent, passion, and financial backing to build top\\-notch, industry\\-changing cloud\\-based applications and platforms? At Veeva, we build enterprise cloud technology that powers the biggest names in pharmaceuticals, biotech, consumer goods, chemical \\& cosmetics industries. Our customers make vaccines, life\\-saving medicines, and life\\-enhancing products that make a difference in everyday lives. Our technology has transformed these industries, enabling them to get critical products and services to market faster. Our products empower our customers in the fight against global pandemics like COVID\\-19\\.
   

  

 Veeva Systems is expanding its global footprint with a new development centre in Hyderabad, India. You will be working on cutting\\-edge technologies such as Cucumber, AWS(EC2, S3, Workspace), Visual Regression \\& DevOps Tools, playing a pivotal role in developing/maintaining automating tests to support our ever\\-growing suite of regression tests. * Good working knowledge of CI/CD tools (Git, Jenkins, JIRA)

* Bachelor's or Masters' Degree in computer engineering, computer science, electrical engineering, or similar
* Experience with applicable software and programs including Kotlin, Swift, JAVA, and React.

* A natural problem solver and critical thinker.
* Comfortable debugging and working with object\\-oriented programming.

****Nice to Have****

title: Senior Software Engineer II - Video Editor
description:
Goldcast (a Cvent company) is an AI\\-powered B2B Video Content Platform that enables marketers to put video content at the heart of the customer journey. We’re building a
 **browser\\-based, frame\\-accurate video editor** 
 that rivals desktop apps, using cutting\\-edge tech like WebCodecs, WASM, and Remotion.
 



  





 Founded in mid\\-2020 at Harvard Business School, we have 400\\+ customers across tech, professional services, manufacturing, and finance to deliver engaging video content \\& digital events.
 



  





**Role Overview**




 As a Senior Software Engineer II specializing in video editing frameworks, you'll lead our effort to build a
 **frame\\-perfect, multi\\-track video editor** 
 in the browser, combining Remotion for UX and WebCodecs/WASM for
 **low\\-level video processing** 
 . You will extend Remotion’s React layer for UX to enable
 **professional\\-grade editing features** 
 (precise cuts, real\\-time previews, GPU\\-accelerated effects) while navigating browser limitations.
 



  





**What You’ll Do**



* Lead the architecture and development a frame\\-perfect browser\\-based video editing framework (supporting cuts, transitions, and multi\\-track compositing).
* Design and implement real\\-time previews using WebGL, OffscreenCanvas, or WebCodecs that mirror final rendering pixel\\-perfectly.
* Identify performance bottlenecks and optimize rendering pipelines, with particular attention to input latency and animation fluidity
* Integrate WebAssembly (WASM) for performance\\-critical tasks such as frame\\-accurate video decoding, image processing and audio syncing.
* Design the timeline core (playhead scrubbing, layer stacking, non\\-destructive edits).
* Architect a component system on top of Remotion to bypass its limitations for core video processing.
* Develop tools to measure and enforce frame\\-perfect exports (e.g., automated diffing between preview and render).
* Solve browser\\-specific challenges (memory limits, GPU acceleration, audio/video sync drift).
* Collaborate with design and product teams to create intuitive video editing experiences
* Mentor other engineers on browser media APIs and performance optimization.



  





  





**What We Seek**



* 7\\+ years of frontend engineering experience with at least
 **2\\+ years in video/audio processing on the browser.**
* Deep expertise in browser media APIs: WebCodecs, Web Audio, MediaStream, Canvas.
* Experience with frame\\-accurate video manipulation (e.g., FFmpeg.wasm, custom WebCodecs pipelines).
* Proven ability to optimize JavaScript/WebAssembly performance (e.g., reducing input latency, smoothing animations).
* Experience pushing Remotion or similar frameworks beyond its limits for performance critical tasks. e.g., replacing with WebCodecs, optimizing for multi\\-track editing.
* Experience building browser\\-based media editing or processing tools
* Shipped WASM\\-based video/audio tools to production (e.g., FFmpeg.wasm, Rust/C\\+\\+ for WebAssembly).
* Experience with GPU acceleration and hardware\\-accelerated graphics in browser environments.



  





**Preferred Qualifications**



* Experience with WebGL/Canvas optimizations for real\\-time video previews.
* Knowledge of video codecs (H.264, VP9\\) and compression algorithms and browser encoding limitations.
* Experience working with HLS, WebRTC \\& FFmpeg.
* Deep understanding of performance challenges in browser environments (e.g., garbage collection pauses, GPU memory leaks)
* Experience with WebGL\\-based motion graphics (e.g., shaders for transitions, GPU\\-accelerated text animations).
* Familiarity with rendering loops, frame timing, and smooth animation techniques
* Built developer tools for performance profiling (e.g., frame timings, memory snapshots).



  





**This role isn’t a fit if you:**



* Prefer working with stable APIs (we’re pioneering uncharted browser capabilities).
* Haven’t debugged
 **frame drops** 
 or
 **audio drift** 
 in a media app.



  





**What We Offer**



* Greenfield opportunity to define the architecture of a bleeding\\-edge browser video stack.
* Work with rare expertise (WebCodecs, WASM, Remotion internals) at scale.
* Opportunity to work with a best\\-in\\-class product, engineering, and design team
* An incredible culture blending innovation, collaboration, and technical excellence
* Competitive salary and benefits package
* Hybrid work environment

title: Speaker Diarization AI Researcher
description:
**Company Description** 








 Chordy.ai transforms conversations into actionable, searchable memories. It captures spoken interactions and generates transcripts, summaries, decisions, and action items that are connected to people and topics, building context over time. This innovative platform allows users to retrieve key information using queries like “What did we decide last time?” or “What did I promise?” to access past decisions and commitments. Designed to enhance focus and productivity, Chordy.ai empowers users with precise reminders and tools for seamless follow\\-through.
 



  





**Role Description** 








 This is a full\\-time remote role for a Speaker Diarization AI Researcher. The researcher will focus on designing and implementing advanced algorithms for speaker identification and segmentation to improve the speaker diarization models. Day\\-to\\-day tasks include conducting research on state\\-of\\-the\\-art methods, developing and optimizing machine learning models, processing datasets, collaborating with cross\\-functional teams to integrate new techniques into the company's product offerings, and contributing to publications and presentations in the field of AI and machine learning.
 



  





**Qualifications** 







* Experience with machine learning and artificial intelligence, particularly in speaker diarization, natural language processing, and audio signal processing
* Proficiency in Python and frameworks such as TensorFlow or PyTorch
* Strong knowledge of algorithms, statistical modeling, and data analysis techniques
* Prior experience working with large\\-scale audio datasets and deep learning methods
* Excellent problem\\-solving skills with the ability to work independently in a remote environment
* Proven ability to contribute to scientific publications or present at industry events is a plus
* Educational background in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Machine Learning, or a related field—advanced degrees (Master's or Ph.D.) preferred
